steps.title: Rewrite each radical with fractional exponents
steps.content:
A cube root is a power of $\frac{1}{3}$, and a fourth root is a power of $\frac{1}{4}$:
$$\sqrt[3]{126n}=(126n)^{1/3}$$
$$\sqrt[4]{126n}=(126n)^{1/4}$$

steps.title: Apply the power rule
steps.content:
The second factor is squared, so multiply its exponent by $2$:
$$(\sqrt[4]{126n})^2=((126n)^{1/4})^2=(126n)^{2/4}=(126n)^{1/2}$$

steps.title: Combine powers with the same base
steps.content:
Now multiply the two powers of $126n$ by adding their exponents:
$$(126n)^{1/3}(126n)^{1/2}=(126n)^{1/3+1/2}=(126n)^{5/6}$$

steps.title: Match the exponent
steps.content:
The expression is equivalent to $(126n)^{18x}$, so set the exponents equal:
$$18x=\frac{5}{6}$$
Divide by $18$:
$$x=\frac{5}{6}\cdot\frac{1}{18}=\frac{5}{108}$$
The answer is 5/108.
